reasoning: The LLM fidelity analysis indicates a perfectly clean document with scores of 1.0 across the board. However, the automated programmatic detectors found a major structural defect: flattened sub-lists in a table. Since count-based routing rules dictate that 1-5 major defects require human review, the document must be routed to HUMAN_REVIEW for structural remediation.

concerns.description: Flattened sub-lists: 4 item(s) contain nested sub-items (a/b/c or bullets) as plain text instead of structured children
concerns.analysis: flattened_sublists=4. Examples: p1 table: • Cost share payments received., • Funds expended.; p1 table: • Review inflow development., • Review model calibration., • Discuss model calibration and limitati; p1 table: • Present model simulation results., • Present model forecast results.. Sub-items embedded as newline-delimited text lose semantic nesting in HTML — screen readers can't navigate the hierarchy.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "10:00-10:05"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=0. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

action_items: Review the table and structure the newline-delimited bullet points into proper nested HTML lists.
Ensure table headers contain appropriate column labels rather than time values if applicable.
